author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
verses:
svabhaktiyoga⋅lāsinaṁ sadā vraje vihāriṇaṁ
hari⋅priyā⋅gaṇāgragaṁ śacī⋅suta⋅priyeśvaram
sarādhā⋅kṛṣṇasevana⋅prakāśakaṁ mahāśayaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

navojjvalādi⋅bhāvanā⋅vidhāna⋅karmapāragaṁ
vicitra⋅gaura⋅bhakti⋅sindhu⋅rasabhaṅga⋅lāsinam
surāga⋅mārga⋅darśakaṁ vrajādi⋅vāsa⋅dāyakaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

śacī⋅sutāṅghrisāra⋅bhakta⋅vṛnda⋅vandya⋅gauravaṁ
gaurabhāva⋅cittapadma⋅madhya⋅kṛṣṇa⋅suvallabham
mukunda⋅gaurarūpiṇaṁ sva⋅bhāva⋅dharma⋅dāyakaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

nikuñja⋅sevanādika⋅prakāśanaika⋅kāraṇaṁ
sadā sakhīrati⋅pradaṁ mahārasa⋅svarūpakaṁ
sadāśritāṅghri⋅puṇḍarīka⋅pradaṁ sadguruṁ varaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

mahāprabhormahārasa⋅prakāśanāṅkuraṁ priyaṁ
sadā mahārasāṅkura⋅prakāśanādi⋅vāsanāṁ
mahāprabhorvrajāṅganādi⋅bhāva⋅moda⋅kārakaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

dvijendra⋅vṛnda⋅vandya⋅pādayugma⋅bhakti⋅vardhakaṁ
nijeṣu rādhikātmatā⋅vapuḥ⋅prakāśanāgraham
aśeṣa⋅bhaktiśāstra⋅śikṣayojjvalāmṛta⋅pradaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

mudā nija⋅priyādika⋅svapāda⋅padma⋅sīdhubhi
rmahārasārṇavāmṛta⋅pradeṣṭa⋅gaurabhaktidam
sadāṣṭa⋅sāttvikānvitaṁ nijeṣṭa⋅bhakti⋅dāyakaṁ
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

jadīya⋅rītirāga⋅raṅgabhaṅga⋅digdha⋅mānaso
naro‛pi jāti tūrṇameva nāryabhāva⋅bhājanam
tamujjvalākta⋅cittametu citta⋅matta⋅ṣaṭpado⋅
bhajāmyahaṁ gadādharaṁ supaṇḍitaṁ guruṁ prabhum

mahārasāmṛta-pradaṁ sadā gadādharāṣṭakaṁ
paṭhet tu jaḥ subhaktito vrajāṅganā⋅gaṇotsavam
śacītanuja⋅pāda⋅padma⋅bhaktiratna⋅yogyatāṁ
labheta rādhikā⋅gadādharāṅghri⋅padma⋅sevayā
en_translation:
I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who always delights in the practice of bhakti-yoga and resides in Vraja, who is foremost among the beloved associates of Hari and dear to the Son of Śacī, who reveals the service of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, and who is a great personality.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who is expert in arranging the emotions of the newly brilliant and others, who delights in the varied rasas of the ocean of Gaura-bhakti, who guides us on the path of intense devotional attachment, and bestows residence in Vraja and other holy places.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who is honored by the multitude of devotees whose essence is the lotus feet of Śacī’s Son, who is most beloved by Kṛṣṇa, who resides in the lotus of Gaura’s heart, who embodies Mukunda in Gaura’s form, and who bestows one's inherent spiritual nature.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who is the sole cause for revealing the service of the groves and other such things, who always gives the ecstatic love of the sakhīs, who is the embodiment of great rasa, who is the most excellent true guru, who bestows the lotus feet of those always surrendered to him.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who is the beloved sprout revealing Mahāprabhu’s great rasa, who always has the desire to reveal the sprout of great rasa, who brings joy in the mood of the gopīs of Vraja and others to Mahāprabhu.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, who enhances devotion to the lotus feet honored by the multitude of best brāhmaṇas, who insists on revealing his own body as the soul of Rādhikā, who bestows the bright nectar through teaching all the scriptures of devotion.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, by whose sweet nectar from the lotus feet of his beloved ones, he bestows Gaura-bhakti, the great rasa-ocean nectar, who is always accompanied by the eight bodily transformations of ecstatic love, who bestows devotion to his chosen Deity.

I worship Gadādhara, the greatly learned guru and master, whose mind is steeped in the waves and colors of his unique sweet love; even a man whose mind is thus infused quickly attains the qualification for the mood of a woman; let my mind, like a maddened bee, approach him, whose heart is imbued with ujjvala-rasa.

Whoever reads these eight verses on Gadādhara, which always bestow the nectar of great rasa, with great devotion, which describe the festival of the gopīs of Vraja, will attain the eligibility for the jewel of devotion to the lotus feet of Śacī’s Son, through the service of the lotus feet of Rādhikā and Gadādhara.
